hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.label: Hilbert annihilator range criterion
hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.reason: For a densely defined closed trace map R between Hilbert spaces, closure Ran(R^*)=(ker R)^perp.  Hence E_active factors through the interval trace iff ker R is annihilated by E_active.

finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.label: finite active block factorization
finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.reason: Current Galerkin active blocks satisfy E_active=C_sample R_global to roundoff.  This is finite evidence for, not proof of, the continuum annihilator condition.

compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.label: compactness contradiction reduction
compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.reason: Failure of ker R subset ker E_active is equivalent to an A-normalized sequence with R f_n -> 0 and active source bounded below.  The high-block elliptic/Mosco theorem is the compactness mechanism needed to pass such a sequence to a continuum limit.

closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.label: closed-trace active unique continuation
closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.reason: If Lambda_a(f)=0 on I, the Green representation annihilates every scalar active source row.  Therefore E_active f=0.

continuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilitySt.label: continuum active trace range compatibility
continuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilitySt.reason: The closed-trace active unique-continuation theorem proves ker R subset ker E_active.  The Hilbert annihilator criterion then gives E_active in closure Ran(R^*).

endpointFredholmCompatibilityFromActiveR.label: endpoint Fredholm compatibility from active range
endpointFredholmCompatibilityFromActiveR.reason: Since E_active lies in closure Ran(R^*), the endpoint vector b(d_u) annihilates ker(M^T), so b(d_u) lies in Range(M).  In the interval endpoint theorem this compatibility is also vacuous because the active endpoint map has full row rank.

volterraSturmLagrangeIdentityStatus.label: Volterra/Sturm Lagrange identity
volterraSturmLagrangeIdentityStatus.reason: Imported from the symbolic Lagrange identity theorem: D_a B_P[K,f]=K P f-f P^*K.

distributionalInteriorGreenSourceStatus.label: distributional interior Green source
distributionalInteriorGreenSourceStatus.reason: Imported from the symbolic jump theorem: every local active source row has a unique jump vector, since the jump map is triangular with nonzero leading coefficient.

exactEndpointFredholmReductionStatus.label: exact endpoint Fredholm reduction
exactEndpointFredholmReductionStatus.reason: The exact fundamental matrix gives the endpoint equation Mz=-b(ell), with the usual finite-dimensional Fredholm alternative.

formalGreenAnnihilationImplicationStatus.label: formal Green annihilation implication
formalGreenAnnihilationImplicationStatus.reason: For each active scalar source row ell, the solved Green coefficient gives ell(f)=int_I K_ell(a)Lambda_a(f)da.

closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.label: closed-trace active unique continuation
closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.reason: If Lambda_a(f)=0 on I, the Green representation annihilates every scalar active source row.  Therefore E_active f=0.

endpointRangeTheorem.theoremName: adjoint Green endpoint range interval theorem

exactFundamentalMatrixEndpointMapStatus.label: exact fundamental-matrix endpoint map
exactFundamentalMatrixEndpointMapStatus.reason: For analytic coefficients with nonzero leading coefficient, the homogeneous adjoint equation P^*K=0 has a fundamental matrix Phi(a,t).  Endpoint concomitants are finite-dimensional linear functions of the initial vector z.

endpointFredholmRangeAlternativeStatus.label: endpoint Fredholm range alternative
endpointFredholmRangeAlternativeStatus.reason: The endpoint equation Mz=-b(d) is solvable iff b(d) lies in Range(M), equivalently iff every vector in ker(M^*) annihilates b(d).

endpointFullActiveRowRankStatus.label: endpoint map has full active row rank
endpointFullActiveRowRankStatus.reason: The synchronized endpoint interval/Krawczyk certificate keeps the exact endpoint map in the persistent Pluecker chart, so M is onto the active endpoint row space.

endpointGreenBvpSolvabilityStatus.label: endpoint Green BVP solvable for every active source row
endpointGreenBvpSolvabilityStatus.reason: The jump theorem supplies the interior source, and full active row rank makes the endpoint Fredholm compatibility condition vacuous.

uniformTraceDualBoundFromCompactnessStat.label: uniform trace-dual bound on the Green solution family
uniformTraceDualBoundFromCompactnessStat.reason: The source window is compact, the source rows depend continuously on the source parameter, and the endpoint right inverse is bounded by the positive singular margin of the interval full-rank certificate.

continuumActiveRangeCompatibilityStatus.label: continuum active endpoint compatibility
continuumActiveRangeCompatibilityStatus.reason: Compatibility is automatic for the active endpoint equation because the endpoint map is onto the active row space.

definitions.solution: z(d)=-M^+b(d), using the bounded right inverse of M

formalProof: The jump theorem reduces P^*K=ell to homogeneous adjoint ODEs on the two sides of s0 plus a fixed jump Delta(ell).
The homogeneous adjoint ODE has an exact fundamental matrix, so every Green candidate is parameterized by z.
Substituting the endpoint jets into the active concomitant gives Mz+b(ell).
The synchronized interval/Krawczyk certificate proves M has full active row rank.  Hence Mz=-b(ell) is solvable for every active source row ell.
The bounded right inverse and compact source window give uniform trace-dual bounds for the selected Green family.

exactEquivalences: E_active in closure Ran(R^*) iff ker R subset ker E_active.
Failure iff there are f_n with ||f_n||_A=1, ||R f_n|| -> 0, and ||E_active f_n|| >= delta.
If compactness gives f_n -> f locally and R f_n -> 0, then Lambda_a(f)=0 on I.
The remaining unique-continuation lemma must show the same limit has E_active f=0, contradicting the lower active source bound.
formalProof: Let R:H_A->L^2(I) be the closed interval trace operator and let E_active:H_A->U_delta be the active source map.
By the closed-range/annihilator identity, a bounded active source row factors through R precisely when it vanishes on ker R.
If vanishing fails, normalize a sequence in the bad set.  The commuted Sturm elliptic estimate plus Mosco high-block exhaustion is the compactness engine producing a nonzero closed-trace limit.

The trace limit satisfies Lambda_a(f)=0 on the whole active interval.  The remaining analytic input is unique continuation in the Volterra/Sturm branch that forces the active source row of such a limit to vanish.

That contradiction gives ker R subset ker E_active.  Hence E_active is in closure Ran(R^*), giving the continuum trace-to-source representation and the endpoint Fredholm compatibility b(d_u) in Range(M).
